title: collomia linearis
intro: Collomia linearis, also known as the narrow-leaved collomia or bluebell bellflower, is a perennial plant species in the phlox family (Polemoniaceae) native to the western United States and southwestern Canada. Traditionally, Collomia linearis has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Collomia linearis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
